# Ocean Tides

Tides are the periodic rise and fall of sea levels caused by the gravitational forces of the Moon and the Sun combined with the rotation of the Earth.

## Spring and neap tides

Spring tides occur when the Sun, Moon, and Earth are aligned, producing the largest tidal range. Neap tides happen when the Sun and Moon are at right angles relative to Earth, producing the smallest tidal range.

## The role of the Moon

The Moon is the dominant driver of tides because it is far closer to Earth than the Sun. Its gravitational pull creates two tidal bulges, one facing the Moon and one on the opposite side of the planet.
